items.body: The 126th U.S. Open returns to Shinnecock Hills on Long Island. Scottie Scheffler is the betting favorite. A win would complete his career Grand Slam on his 30th birthday Sunday. Rory McIlroy, defending champion J.J. Spaun, and 2018 winner Brooks Koepka headline a 156-player field. Shinnecock is a demanding par-70 test that rewards precision, patience, and solid iron play.

items.label: Scottie Scheffler (+550 to +567):
items.body: The world No. 1 and clear betting favorite. He needs a U.S. Open title to complete the career Grand Slam.

items.label: Rory McIlroy (+1135 to +1200):
items.body: The 2026 Masters champion and 2011 U.S. Open winner. He arrives in strong form at one of America's toughest tests.

items.label: Jon Rahm (+1300 to +1752):
items.body: A proven major champion with the ball striking to stay in contention on a course that punishes mistakes.

items.label: Tommy Fleetwood (+2000 to +2464):
items.body: He shot a final-round 63 here in 2018 and remains one of the best course fits in the field.

items.label: Xander Schauffele (+2000 to +2281):
items.body: A two-time major winner who has never finished worse than T14 at the U.S. Open.

items.label: Cameron Young (+2028 to +2200):
items.body: The world No. 3 and PLAYERS champion is playing his best golf, including a T4 at last year's U.S. Open.

items.label: Ludvig Aberg (+2200 to +2500):
items.body: An elite ball striker whose accuracy fits Shinnecock's narrow fairways and fast greens.

items.label: Brooks Koepka (+2500 to +3348):
items.body: Won the U.S. Open here in 2018 and successfully defended the title that same year.

items.label: Matt Fitzpatrick (+2700 to +4067):
items.body: The 2022 U.S. Open champion is having a strong 2026 season.

items.label: Venue:
items.body: Shinnecock Hills Golf Club in Southampton, New York.

items.label: Established:
items.body: Founded in 1891. Redesigned by William Flynn in the 1930s. This is the sixth U.S. Open at Shinnecock.

items.label: Defending Champion:
items.body: J.J. Spaun won the 2025 U.S. Open at Oakmont. He was the only player under par for the week.

items.label: Tradition:
items.body: Run by the USGA since 1895. Thousands of players qualify each year through local and final qualifying.

items.label: Course:
items.body: Shinnecock Hills Golf Club, Southampton, New York.

items.label: Dates:
items.body: June 18 through June 21, 2026.

items.label: Purse:
items.body: $21.5 million. The 2026 figure is usually confirmed during tournament week.

items.label: Format:
items.body: 156-player field, 72-hole stroke play. The low 60 and ties make the cut.

items.label: Course Profile:
items.body: 7,440 yards, par 70. Fast fairways, small guarded greens, and just two par-5s.

items.label: Coverage:
items.body: Rounds air on USA Network, NBC, Peacock, and Golf Channel. Thursday starts at 6:30 a.m. ET on USA Network. Weekend coverage moves to NBC from noon. Golf Channel's Live From the U.S. Open runs throughout the week.

items.label: Event Window:
items.body: Competition runs June 18 through June 21, 2026.
